description:
Triage: ready-for-agent

## Problem Statement

Beadsmith currently shows a static issue explorer shell: the sidebar advertises All, Ready, Blocked, and lifecycle state choices, while the search field advertises issue search. Those controls are not interactive, so the user cannot narrow the Issue List to the Beadwork view they care about or search within that view. This makes Beadsmith less useful as a work browser because the user must visually scan every Issue rather than switching to a focused Issue List View.

## Solution

Make the existing sidebar and search chrome functional. The Issue List will expose mutually exclusive Issue List Views for All Issues, Ready, Blocked, and each currently supported Beadwork Issue Status: open, in_progress, closed, and deferred. All Issues and status-specific views will be based on the full Beadwork issue list, while Ready and Blocked will use Beadwork's authoritative CLI views so Beadsmith does not reimplement workflow semantics. Local Issue Search will narrow the active Issue List View using a case-insensitive tokenized AND search over Issue ID, title, and raw description.

## User Stories

1. As a Beadsmith user, I want to click All, so that I can see every Issue in the Current Workspace.
2. As a Beadsmith user, I want All to include every Beadwork Issue across stored statuses, so that it matches the project glossary.
3. As a Beadsmith user, I want All to remain the default Issue List View, so that Beadsmith opens with the same broad overview it has today.
4. As a Beadsmith user, I want to click Ready, so that I can see the Issues Beadwork considers available to work next.
5. As a Beadsmith user, I want Ready to use Beadwork's own ready rules, so that Beadsmith does not disagree with `bw ready`.
6. As a Beadsmith user, I want to click Blocked, so that I can see Issues Beadwork considers blocked by unresolved blockers.
7. As a Beadsmith user, I want Blocked to use Beadwork's own blocked rules, so that Beadsmith does not invent a different meaning of blocked work.
8. As a Beadsmith user, I want to click Open, so that I can focus on Issues whose stored Issue Status is open.
9. As a Beadsmith user, I want to click In Progress, so that I can focus on Issues whose stored Issue Status is in progress.
10. As a Beadsmith user, I want to click Closed, so that I can review completed Issues.
11. As a Beadsmith user, I want to click Deferred, so that I can review Issues whose stored Issue Status is deferred.
12. As a Beadsmith user, I want Issue List Views to be selectable one at a time, so that I never have to understand combined view/status filtering rules.
13. As a Beadsmith user, I want views to be allowed to overlap, so that an Issue can appear wherever Beadwork's different questions say it belongs.
14. As a Beadsmith user, I want the sidebar group currently labeled States to be labeled Status, so that it matches Beadwork lifecycle terminology.
15. As a Beadsmith user, I want the UI label In Progress instead of In-Progress, so that the status reads naturally.
16. As a Beadsmith user, I want one active sidebar item across both sidebar groups, so that I can clearly tell which Issue List View is active.
17. As a Beadsmith user, I want clicking the active sidebar item again to do nothing, so that repeated clicks are not surprising.
18. As a Beadsmith user, I want each sidebar item to show its base count, so that I can understand how much work exists in each Issue List View.
19. As a Beadsmith user, I want zero-count sidebar items to show 0, so that I know the view was loaded and is empty.
20. As a Beadsmith user, I want zero-count sidebar items to remain clickable, so that I can see the correct empty state for that view.
21. As a Beadsmith user, I want sidebar counts to ignore the current search query, so that counts do not jump around while I type.
22. As a Beadsmith user, I want Ready and Blocked counts to reflect Beadwork's authoritative command results, so that counts match Beadwork workflow semantics.
23. As a Beadsmith user, I want status counts to come from All Issues, so that stored lifecycle counts are fast and consistent.
24. As a Beadsmith user, I want view switching to feel instant after load, so that moving between Issue List Views is lightweight.
25. As a Beadsmith user, I want the Issue Explorer to fail as a whole if the required Beadwork data cannot be loaded, so that I am not shown partial or semantically misleading results.
26. As a Beadsmith user, I want Ready to preserve Beadwork's command ordering, so that Beadwork's prioritization and due-date behavior remain visible.
27. As a Beadsmith user, I want Blocked to preserve Beadwork's command ordering, so that Beadsmith does not reorder Beadwork-authored output.
28. As a Beadsmith user, I want All and status views to preserve the underlying Beadwork list ordering, so that Beadsmith does not impose a competing sort.
29. As a Beadsmith user, I want search to narrow the active Issue List View without changing its order, so that search only removes non-matches.
30. As a Beadsmith user, I want the search field to be enabled after a successful load, so that I can search even when the active view currently has zero rows.
31. As a Beadsmith user, I want the search field to remain disabled during loading or failure, so that I do not interact with unavailable data.
32. As a Beadsmith user, I want loading copy to reflect that Beadsmith is loading issue views, so that the wait matches the All, Ready, and Blocked preload.
33. As a Beadsmith user, I want the search placeholder to remain "Search issues...", so that the existing UI stays simple.
34. As a Beadsmith user, I want the existing Cmd+F hint to remain visible, so that the UI can support that future shortcut without layout churn.
35. As a keyboard user, I want sidebar Issue List View buttons to be reachable by normal Tab navigation and activatable with Enter or Space, so that the feature remains basically accessible even without custom shortcuts.
36. As a Beadsmith user, I want search to be scoped to the active Issue List View, so that Ready search only searches Ready and Closed search only searches Closed.
37. As a Beadsmith user, I want search to look at Issue ID, so that I can find an Issue when I know its identifier.
38. As a Beadsmith user, I want search to look at Issue title, so that I can find Issues by their visible summary.
39. As a Beadsmith user, I want search to look at raw Issue description, so that I can find Issues by the main body of their Beadwork content.
40. As a Beadsmith user, I want search not to include comments in this phase, so that results are not explained by hidden or noisy content.
41. As a Beadsmith user, I want search to be case-insensitive, so that capitalization does not matter.
42. As a Beadsmith user, I want search to split my query into whitespace-separated tokens, so that I can type multiple words naturally.
43. As a Beadsmith user, I want every search token to be required, so that multi-word searches narrow results instead of broadening them.
44. As a Beadsmith user, I want partial tokens to match, so that results narrow progressively while I type.
45. As a Beadsmith user, I want punctuation inside a search token to be preserved, so that Issue IDs such as `bsm-123` work naturally.
46. As a Beadsmith user, I want search to update immediately as I type, so that I get fast local feedback.
47. As a Beadsmith user, I want the search query to persist when I switch Issue List Views, so that I can compare the same search across All, Ready, Blocked, and status views.
48. As a Beadsmith user, I want the active Issue List View and search query not to persist across app reloads, so that Beadsmith starts cleanly in All each time.
49. As a Beadsmith user, I want switching Issue List Views to reset the Issue List scroll position to the top, so that a new view does not open in the middle of its list.
50. As a Beadsmith user, I want no new active-view header near the search bar, so that the existing layout remains unchanged.
51. As a Beadsmith user, I want the active sidebar item and count to be the only active-view indicators, so that the list panel stays focused on search and rows.
52. As a Beadsmith user, I want the selected Issue to clear when view or search changes hide it, so that the Issue Detail never shows an Issue absent from the visible Issue List.
53. As a Beadsmith user, I want a cleared selection to stay cleared even if the Issue becomes visible again, so that the detail pane does not unexpectedly repopulate.
54. As a Beadsmith user, I want selecting an Issue that remains visible to continue showing its Issue Detail, so that narrowing unrelated rows does not disrupt my current reading.
55. As a Beadsmith user, I want the true-empty workspace copy to remain distinct, so that I can tell when Beadwork returned no Issues at all.
56. As a Beadsmith user, I want true-empty workspace copy to take precedence over view/search empty copy, so that an empty Current Workspace is never presented as merely a filtered result.
57. As a Beadsmith user, I want a base-empty Issue List View to explain that the selected view has no Issues, so that Ready, Blocked, and status views are understandable when their base count is zero.
58. As a Beadsmith user, I want a filtered-empty view to say "No matching issues", so that I can tell the Current Workspace has Issues but this view/search has none.
59. As a Beadsmith user, I want a no-search filtered-empty state to name the active view, so that `No issues in Ready.` is clear.
60. As a Beadsmith user, I want a search filtered-empty state to include my query, so that `No issues in Closed match "migration".` is clear.
61. As a Beadsmith user, I want dependency chips and Issue Detail dependency display to stay consistent across views, so that Blocked does not introduce special dependency UI in this phase.
62. As a Beadsmith user, I want Issue rows and Issue Detail rendering to remain consistent whether an Issue appears through All, Ready, Blocked, or a status view, so that views feel like lenses over the same Beadwork data.
63. As a Beadsmith user, I want Beadsmith to remain a desktop client for Beadwork rather than a second issue tracker, so that Beadwork remains the source of truth.
## Implementation Decisions

- The feature will make the existing sidebar and Issue List search controls functional rather than introducing a new navigation surface.
- Sidebar entries are Issue List Views, not combinable filters. Only one sidebar item is active at a time across the Views and Status groups.
- Issue List Views are selectable lenses and may overlap. They are not partitions of the workspace.
- All Issues means every Beadwork Issue in the Current Workspace across Beadwork's stored statuses.
- Ready is loaded from Beadwork's authoritative ready view using `bw ready --json`.
- Blocked is loaded from Beadwork's authoritative blocked view using `bw blocked --json`.
- Beadwork's blocked JSON may include `open_blockers`, but Beadsmith does not need special open-blocker presentation in this slice. The native adapter should ignore or drop `open_blockers` and keep the frontend Issue contract clean. The normal `blocked_by` issue data remains the dependency display source; if Beadsmith later needs open-only blocker presentation, it can reconstruct that from loaded Issue data.
- Status-specific Issue List Views are derived locally by slicing All Issues by the four currently supported stored Issue Status values: open, in_progress, closed, and deferred.
- Ready and Blocked are not reimplemented locally from `blockedBy`, status, parent, or deferral fields.
- All, Ready, and Blocked data are preloaded during Issue Explorer load so sidebar switching is immediate and counts are available.
- The native/RPC boundary should expose one combined command, tentatively named `load_issue_explorer_data`, returning the Current Workspace path plus All, Ready, and Blocked issue collections. The frontend observes one all-or-nothing load result instead of coordinating multiple Taurpc calls.
- The successful loaded frontend state should contain all three preloaded collections, even when they are empty. True-empty workspace UI is derived from the All Issues base count being `0`, not from a separate failed or unavailable state.
- Ready and Blocked issues are normalized into the same frontend Issue contract as All Issues. View-specific metadata from command output, such as `open_blockers`, is ignored or dropped for this slice.
- If any required load step fails, the Issue Explorer shows the failure state as a whole. There are no partial per-view errors and no local fallback for Ready or Blocked.
- Beadsmith preserves Beadwork command ordering for every base Issue List View. Search narrows the existing order but does not reorder matches. Different views may therefore visibly jump between different natural orderings, such as All/status list ordering versus Ready command ordering.
- The sidebar group label States becomes Status, and every visible label for `in_progress` becomes In Progress, including sidebar labels and row/status badges.
- Code terminology should use status instead of state when referring to the Beadwork lifecycle field. Internal status IDs should match Beadwork stored values, including `in_progress`; display labels may be humanized separately.
- Sidebar counts show each base Issue List View count before search is applied. Counts include zero and are never hidden because of zero values. Counts are derived from the loaded collections: All, Ready, and Blocked use their preloaded collection lengths; status counts are derived by filtering All Issues by stored status.
- Zero-count views remain selectable and show their empty state, except that a truly empty workspace takes precedence as described below.
- Clicking the currently active sidebar item is a no-op.
- Switching Issue List Views resets the Issue List scroll position to the top.
- No new active-view header or label is added near the search bar.
- The search placeholder remains "Search issues...". Loading copy should describe the combined issue-view load, for example "Loading issue views…", rather than implying only one list is loading.
- The existing Cmd+F visual hint remains in place, but keyboard shortcut behavior is out of scope. Sidebar Issue List View controls must be real enabled buttons with normal focus order and Enter/Space activation.
- Issue Search is local to the loaded data and scoped to the active Issue List View.
- Issue Search covers Issue ID, title, and raw description only.
- Issue Search is case-insensitive, tokenized by trimmed whitespace, and uses AND semantics: every query token must match somewhere in the searchable Issue text.
- Issue Search uses substring matching for partial tokens.
- Issue Search preserves punctuation inside tokens.
- Issue Search updates immediately as the user types and is not debounced. Empty or whitespace-only search has no tokens and does not narrow the active Issue List View.
- Search stays enabled after a successful load, including true-empty and filtered-empty states. Search stays disabled during loading and failure.
- The search query persists across Issue List View switches but not across app reloads. Persisting the query means only the text persists; visible rows are recomputed against the newly active Issue List View.
- If the currently selected Issue is hidden by a view or search change, selection is cleared. View switching only clears selection when the selected Issue is not visible in the new active view/search result. Cleared selection is not restored automatically when the Issue becomes visible again; this avoids ghost selections where the detail pane unexpectedly reappears after later search or view changes.
- The true-empty workspace copy remains separate from view-empty and search-filtered-empty copy. If the All Issues base count is `0`, Beadsmith always shows the true-empty workspace copy regardless of active view or search query.
- If the workspace has at least one Issue and the active view base count is `0`, Beadsmith shows base view-empty copy such as "No issues in Ready." or a more specific equivalent like "No issues are ready."
- If the active view has base Issues but the active search hides them all, filtered-empty copy uses "No matching issues" with view/query-specific supporting text.
- Dependency display remains the same across all views in this phase. Blocked does not get special open-blocker presentation from `open_blockers`.
- The ADR "Use Beadwork CLI views for Ready and Blocked" records the architectural decision to call Beadwork's authoritative Ready and Blocked commands.
- The highest product seam for this feature is the App plus Issue Explorer experience: loading data, selecting Issue List Views in the sidebar, narrowing with Issue Search, and reading the resulting Issue List and Issue Detail.
- The highest technical seam is the App/Issue Explorer integration boundary because the sidebar currently lives in the app shell while search, list rows, and Issue Detail live in the Issue Explorer. Default ownership: App owns the loaded Issue Explorer data and active Issue List View because it owns the sidebar and sidebar counts; it passes the active view, loaded collections, and view-change handler through the integration boundary. Issue Search and selected Issue state may stay at the smallest shared boundary that satisfies the selection-clearing rules. Tests should cover the user-visible integration rather than internal helper structure.
- The native/RPC seam should expose one bundled Issue Explorer load, `load_issue_explorer_data`, rather than requiring the React surface to coordinate multiple Taurpc calls. That load delivers All, Ready, and Blocked authoritative data to the frontend. Status views remain frontend derivations from All Issues.

## Testing Decisions

- Tests should focus on external behavior visible to the user: active sidebar state, counts, rendered rows, search results, empty copy, selection behavior, and real desktop behavior where appropriate.
- Tests should avoid locking implementation details such as helper names, internal reducer structure, or whether Ready/Blocked are joined by IDs versus rendered directly from normalized command output.
- Component tests should cover sidebar click behavior, including that only one sidebar item is active and clicking the active item again is a no-op. Tests should also cover that sidebar controls are enabled buttons with accessible activation semantics.
- Component tests should cover sidebar counts, including zero counts and the rule that counts do not change when search changes.
- Component tests should cover status-specific Issue List Views as slices of All Issues.
- Component or service tests should cover Ready and Blocked coming from command-backed loaded data rather than local readiness/blocking derivation.
- Component tests should cover scoped search over Issue ID, title, and raw description only.
- Component tests should cover case-insensitive tokenized-AND behavior, partial token matching, whitespace splitting, and punctuation-preserving Issue ID searches.
- Component tests should cover that search narrows the active Issue List View and preserves the base order, including empty/whitespace-only search not narrowing the view.
- Component tests should cover that the search query persists across Issue List View switches.
- Component tests should cover that selected Issue Detail clears when view/search hides the selected Issue and does not auto-restore after the Issue becomes visible again.
- Component tests should cover the difference between true-empty copy and filtered-empty copy, including true-empty precedence when All Issues count is `0` and the user selects another view or enters a search query.
- Existing App and Issue Explorer component tests are the closest prior art for sidebar rendering, list rendering, selected Issue Detail behavior, optional metadata, Markdown, comments, and dependencies.
- Existing issue-loader tests are prior art for loading success, empty, and failure states; they should be extended or paralleled for the expanded loaded-data contract, including the combined loading copy.
- Existing issue-view tests are prior art for pure view-model coverage and can be extended if search/view helper logic is factored into pure functions.
- Native adapter tests should follow the existing fake CommandRunner pattern for each new Ready/Blocked command path. Native RPC tests should cover the combined `load_issue_explorer_data` path, including the case where one underlying command fails and the whole explorer load fails. Tests should also cover dropping or ignoring `open_blockers` from Blocked JSON for this slice.
- WebDriver end-to-end coverage should include one small happy-path scenario proving that the real desktop binary supports sidebar view selection and local search. It should not exhaustively test every view/search combination. The fixture should include at least one ready issue, one blocked issue, one closed issue, and one deferred issue so the scenario can prove the views are distinct.
- The WebDriver documentation that currently lists search/filter coverage as out of scope should be updated when this feature ships.

## Out of Scope

- Custom keyboard shortcuts and list keyboard navigation, including implementing Cmd+F or Ctrl+F. Baseline button keyboard accessibility for sidebar controls remains in scope.
- Search-result highlighting in Issue rows or Issue Detail content.
- Workspace switching.
- Command palette behavior.
- Persistent filters or persisted search state across app reloads.
- Server-side grep/search or Beadwork `--grep` integration.
- Combining Issue List Views with Issue Status filters.
- Partial per-view failure states.
- Local fallback derivation for Ready or Blocked.
- Special Blocked-view dependency rendering that emphasizes open blockers only.
- New active-view headers or labels near the search box.
- URL, deep-link, or routing state for active view/search.

## Further Notes

- The existing UI already contains the sidebar groups and search box; this PRD primarily makes that chrome real.
- Beadsmith should continue to use the domain glossary: Issue, Issue List, Issue Detail, Issue Search, Issue List View, Issue Status, All Issues, Ready, Blocked, and Current Workspace. The glossary should distinguish stored status values such as `in_progress` from display labels such as In Progress.
- The feature should respect the existing ADRs: Beadwork remains the source of truth; Beadsmith integrates through Beadwork CLI JSON output; Tauri/Rust owns native access; React owns the UI; Taurpc and Effect remain the frontend/native/service boundary. ADR-0005 records `bw ready --json` and `bw blocked --json` as the authoritative Ready/Blocked commands.
- The scout found the main existing seams in the top-level app shell, Issue Explorer, issue loading service, native RPC layer, Beadwork issue adapter, component tests, and WebDriver issue-list suite.
title: Epic: Filtering and search issue list views
